svg.title: LUNC project snapshot capture
svg.desc: A visual summary of Terra Classic's current positioning as a community-run legacy chain driven by burns, staking, and supply discipline.
svg.text: What Terra Classic Is Now
A community-run legacy chain where burns, staking, and survival matter more than pure expansion
Not Terra 2.0
The CoinMarketCap page in this case maps to Terra Classic
and its token LUNC, not to the newer LUNA asset.
That changes the entire research frame.
Community-run legacy chain
The chain still maintains validators, governance,
staking, and a smaller but active ecosystem.
It trades on resilience, not on fresh-chain novelty.
Current market pitch
Burned supply, bonded staking, zero inflation,
and enough live infrastructure to keep LUNC tradable.
That is the modern LUNC thesis in one line.
Why traders still care
Burns have already removed 444.18B LUNC from supply.
Bonded staking currently absorbs 932.78B LUNC.
The mint module currently reports zero inflation and zero annual provisions.
That combination gives LUNC a live scarcity narrative even without a full ecosystem renaissance.
What the market is really buying
The current bid is not a claim that Terra Classic has become a top growth chain again.
It is a bet that post-burn supply, bonded staking, and improved sentiment can create a sharper repricing than many expect.
That is a narrower story, but it is also a more defensible one.
Sources used for this capture: CoinMarketCap, Terra Classic Money, LuncScan, Terra Classic public-node data.
